summary: Plan-only PR inventory classification for 10 listed PRs. No shared canonical was selected. Open non-security PRs are kept as useful but not merge-ready because each still lacks proof, has stale/unknown merge state, or has unresolved review/maintainer blockers. Security-sensitive refs are routed to central security handling. Already-closed non-security refs are kept closed without closure actions.

actions.reason: Useful related localization work, but no closure or merge is safe in plan mode; keep open for proof/rebase or a future narrowed repair.
Open docs PR still has useful work but unresolved documentation correctness and rebase/proof blockers; keep it open rather than close or merge.
Approval-routing/security-boundary work is out of ProjectClownfish backlog cleanup scope; route this exact item to central OpenClaw security handling.
Focused useful Google Chat fix, but unresolved review/proof/rebase blockers make merge or fixed-by closeout unsafe.
Provider catalog update may be useful, but current model/pricing proof and branch freshness are unresolved; keep open without inventing a canonical.
Useful UI improvement, but unresolved technical review findings and stale/proof blockers prevent merge or closure.
Release automation idea is relevant, but workflow correctness and rebase/proof blockers remain unresolved.
Already closed; preserve historical context only.
Already closed after useful parts were determined mostly implemented on main; keep as historical evidence.
Sandbox/parity work linked to a security-sensitive dependency is out of ProjectClownfish backlog cleanup scope; route this exact item to central OpenClaw security handling.
